message:
test(indexer): regression guards for atomic-swap lexical publish contract

Three new tests pin invariants of the publish_staged_lexical_index
atomic-swap path introduced in
"feat(indexer): atomic swap publish for staged lexical index":

- publish_staged_lexical_index_first_publish_with_no_prior_live_index_uses_plain_rename
  The first-ever publish must short-circuit to plain `fs::rename`
  because renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE) requires both paths to exist;
  otherwise we'd get EEXIST/ENOENT depending on flags.
- publish_staged_lexical_index_retains_stale_in_progress_backup_when_live_present
  When a retained backup from a previous crashed publish is present
  alongside a live index, the next publish must not collide with or
  overwrite the stale backup — unique_lexical_publish_backup_path's
  suffixing is exercised here.
- publish_staged_lexical_index_retains_every_backup_unboundedly_today_pending_retention_cap
  Current contract: every publish retains its prior-live tree as a
  dated backup indefinitely, with no retention cap. This test pins
  that behavior so the future introduction of a retention cap is an
  explicit, test-visible change rather than an accidental deletion.

Uses the existing norm_conv/norm_msg helpers to build a small
realistic rebuild state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
